scene_summary: A corporate meeting at LARP where an earnest employee tries to pivot from February month-end close to March, only to be shut down by the cold boss and interrupted by a chaotic coworker.

signature_lines: just wanted to reiterate that today is the last day of February, right?
given that we are effectively at month end close, I do want to underscore that anything we want reflected in February performance needs to materialize today
observations.interpersonal_note: sets the rigid tone for the meeting; dismisses alternative strategies outright

signature_lines: how can we eliminate blockers, focus on tangibles and pull forward any low handling proof to get us across the finish line
observations.interpersonal_note: tries to salvage the situation with buzzword-heavy suggestions; gets cut off by the chaos agent

signature_lines: is there an alternative strategy where we can functionally push February to March
observations.interpersonal_note: interrupts earnest-intern mid-sentence with a half-formed 'alternative' that immediately self-corrects into chaos

signature_lines: nothing for me on my pardon. nothing for me
observations.interpersonal_note: delivers the punchline — the 'alternative strategy' was just a polite way of saying they're getting fired; speaks in deadpan monotone while chaos erupts around them
